title: Raising $30M in 7 Days — And Why Most Founders Shouldn’t Try It

source.description:
Raising $30M in seven days sounds like success — but according to Filip Kozera, it’s not what actually matters.

In this episode of Talks with Dasha, I sit down with the founder of Wordware to talk about the reality behind fast fundraising, extreme ambition, burnout, and what it really takes to build a $100B+ company.

Filip shares what seven years of rejection, YC rejections, and constant pressure taught him — and why most founders misunderstand success.

We talk about:
• How fundraising actually works — and why it’s an auction
• What raising $30M in 7 days doesn’t fix
• Why product obsession beats hype and branding
• Founder burnout, health, and long-term pressure
• Co-founder relationships, trust, and “founder marriage”
• Why San Francisco makes you richer — and emptier
• The future of AI, knowledge work, and why context matters more than intelligence
• Building Sauna and rethinking how humans work with AI
This is an honest, unfiltered conversation about ambition, money, failure, and building something that lasts 10–20 years — not just long enough assuming that the market is hot.

📖 Read more about Woodward on Forbes: https://www.forbes.com/sites/dariashunina/2024/11/27/how-wordware-secured-30m-seed-in-7-days/

🌐 Learn more about Woodward: https://www.wordware.ai

🔗 Connect with Filip on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/filipkozera/

About the host

Dasha is the host and producer of Talks With Dasha, founder of Women Tech Meetup (10,000+ female founders and women in tech), Venture Capital Contributor at Forbes, and GTM at Puzzle.

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/dashashunina/ 
X: https://x.com/dasha_shunina?s=20
Instagram: @feed_my_unicorn

Timecodes:

00:00 Intro — Why Raising $30M in 7 Days Isn’t the Real Story
01:05 What Happens After a Massive Fundraise
03:40 Fundraising as an Auction: Creating Real FOMO
06:30 High Valuations, Mega-Rounds & Unrealistic Expectations
09:20 Building for $100B+ vs. Building to Sell
12:05 Burnout, Pressure & the Emotional Cost of Long-Term Ambition
15:00 Office, Culture & Treating a Startup Like a Sports Team
18:40 Co-Founder Relationships, Trust & “Founder Marriage”
22:00 Why In-Person Work Still Matters (Zoom Allergy)
25:45 YC Rejections, Failure & What Keeps Founders Going
29:30 AI, Knowledge Work & Why Context Matters More Than Intelligence
34:10 Building Sona: Managing AI Agents Like a Team
38:45 Europe vs San Francisco: Ambition, Money & Tradeoffs
42:30 Dating, Loneliness & Founder Life in SF
46:00 What Success Actually Looks Like After 7+ Years
50:10 Final Reflections on Ambition, People & Meaning
Bonus: Wordware HQ Office Tour

theme.summary: This transcript is fundamentally focused on entrepreneurship, business strategy, and company building. The entire conversation covers Philip's entrepreneurial journey building Worldware, extensively discussing core business concepts including: fundraising strategies and auction techniques ($30M in 7 days), startup scaling and pivoting, business model evolution, team building and company culture, office space as business investment, co-founder relationships and business partnerships, hiring strategies and retention, go-to-market strategy changes, venture capital and investment processes, and practical business advice on everything from negotiation to managing business transitions. While it touches on AI product development (Sonar), these are explored through the lens of business strategy, customer acquisition, and building a successful company. The discussion covers practical business advice on fundraising, team management, company culture, and scaling - all fundamental business topics that align perfectly with the existing business category which already contains extensive content on entrepreneurship, startup strategy, business models, venture capital, and company building frameworks from other successful founders.
